items.title: GPT-5.2 derives a new result in theoretical physics | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has published several notable research papers and findings in early 2026. Key publications include work on chain-of-thought monitorability (showing that reasoning models struggle to control their CoT, which is framed as a safety feature), improvements to instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs via the IH-Challenge framework (which trains models to resist prompt injection and prioritize trusted instructions), and a groundbreaking physics result where GPT-5.2 Pro helped derive and verify non

items.title: openai-python CHANGELOG | GitHub
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been actively updating its API platform through early 2026, with significant model and feature changes. On the model front, GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano were released to the Chat Completions and Responses API — mini targets high-volume workloads with GPT-5.4-class capabilities including tool search and computer use support, while nano is optimized for simple, cost-sensitive tasks. Older GPT-5.1 model variants (Instant, Thinking, Pro) were deprecated as of March 11, 2026, with existin

items.title: Introducing Aardvark: OpenAI's agentic security researcher | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been active with major model releases in early 2026. In February, they released GPT-5.3-Codex (Feb 5) and GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark (Feb 12), a smaller real-time coding model. February also saw the retirement of GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, and o4-mini from ChatGPT on Feb 13. March 2026 brought three new GPT-5 family models: GPT-5.3 Instant for fast everyday tasks, GPT-5.4 Thinking for complex professional workflows, and GPT-5.4 Pro for maximum capability use cases. Additionally, Aardvark

items.title: Deepening our collaboration with the U.S. Department of Energy - OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet:
OpenAI's most significant announcement around April 1, 2026 is the completion of a record-breaking $122 billion funding round at an $852 billion valuation. Key investors include Amazon ($50 billion), Nvidia ($30 billion), and SoftBank ($30 billion), with SoftBank and a16z co-leading the round. For the first time, OpenAI also raised over $3 billion from retail investors through bank channels. A portion of Amazon's investment is contingent on OpenAI going public or reaching AGI milestones.

OpenAI
